communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit 2 (Normal Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs.

2. Misuse and Discipline Records

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present.

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims.

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention.

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy.

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm)

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed.

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches.

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about:
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies)
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting.

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including:
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained
- Procedures for deleting or purging data
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

Sincerely, Brad Bell Upload documents directly: https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sX-2FUeO8LEWs7vXOgof-2Bk4pCOrTRbXFG8wEA64rf4GX56WOSlqIdhhPQFaZPVOj92qw-3D-3DISej_trS7M-2BMuPI4Cjis7opQSk9cJxeH6OwYY-2Fx727IGKtV0Ut2F1LeJQoHsTtutUVCHpmBiIV3Fl-2Fjn46PFXl9vkdR9gPqEs6MALIZEClFBr7QeA4VuIl7cH1KO67SqnRy9-2Bnclr3Kd-2F6aS3yrvzEsqPX68XYpen3VYd4-2FZDXuvdKQP8wh9T-2BatWJxrjEp5zfsabsN0ZqK-2FwU-2BriU-2Ff4IFKHrKYliGZDdcBCVv8sBgsceU2-2FxUV3unhMEoGn44cbPpracuAsaZdxN7oG1JfFmUMcQ0N5zlRg8fnsJavw2M-2FRgPaa5UvEWelYPtypemo9vVLF

This letter is to clarify what information you are seeking from The Town of Normal. 
  
 This email serves as a request to reduce the scope of your request in order to prevent the request from becoming unduly burdensome for our municipality under 5 ILCS 140/3(g). 
  
 Your request includes 10 distinct parts. Below, I have indicated which parts of the request need to reduced. 
  
 9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency 
  
 The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting. 
  
 Needs to be narrowed: These audits are generated monthly, yielding more than 30 audit records. Each of these records on average contains at least 61,000 lines of data that would need to be redacted. We estimate each line of data would take about 20 seconds to review and redact. At this rate we could redact 180 lines of data per hour, meaning each audit record would take 338 hours to review and redact, for a total of at least 10,140 staff hours. 
  
 We are happy to work with you to narrow this request. 
  
 
If you would like for The Town of Normal to proceed with your FOIA request, please provide clarifications within ten (10) days from the date of this email. Otherwise, The Town of Normal will consider your request withdrawn.

You may provide a response to The Town of Normal by any of the following methods:

1) by directly responding to this email.
2) by emailing deputyclerk@normalil.gov to clarify.
3) by regular mail to The Town of Normal 11 Uptown Circle, 2nd floor Normal, Illinois 61761.
4) in person at the Office of the Town Clerk of The Town of Normal 11 Uptown Circle, 2nd floor Normal, Illinois 61761. 
  
 Upon receipt of your clarification, The Town of Normal will further respond to your FOIA Request as required by law, including providing you with an estimate of costs associated with producing the requested records.

If you have any questions, please feel free to reply to this email communication. Thank you for your attention. 
  
 Thank you. 
  
 The Town of Normal Clerk's Office 
  
 Angie Huonker, Town Clerk 
  
 Britni Williams, Chief Deputy Clerk

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #RR-912-2026
communications.communication:
Thank you for explaining the burden, please consider the following amended version of item 9.

9. Please provide the following from the Flock Organization Audit (internal searches) from installation to present:

(a) Monthly total search volume per distinct user/User ID.

(b) Count (and, if manageable, a filtered export) of searches where the purpose/reason/tag/description field is blank, null, empty, consists only of whitespace, or fails to contain (i) at least one English dictionary word of two or more letters and (ii) a string matching an Illinois license‑plate pattern (e.g., three letters + three/four digits or two letters + five digits). Provide the count broken down by month and by user/User ID (per this entire category and not per individual term,) and, if the export is not overly burdensome, include the matching records in machine‑readable (CSV/spreadsheet) format.

If providing the full per‑user breakdown is too burdensome, please at least supply a ranked list of the top 20 users (by User ID/username) with the highest number of blank/throw‑away purpose‑field searches, along with the monthly totals for those users.

If this still returns an unmanageable result please let me know and I will further reduce.

communications.communication:

Dear Brad Bell,

The Town of Normal received a FOIA request from you on 05/15/2026.

This letter is to clarify what information you are seeking from The Town of Normal. 
  
 We are in the process of collecting responsive records. We'll need you to narrow the following in order to avoid unduly burdening the municipality under 5 ILCS 140/3(g): 
  
 - 1. Ideological/Identity Based search records: Please provide keywords you would like us to search by. 
  
 - 9b. Count of searches: Please provide keywords you would like us to search by. 
  
 Each of these would require manual counting rather than report generation which would take several thousand hours of staff time, which would unduly burden the municipality. 
  
 Please let us know how you'd like to narrow your request. 
  
 The Normal PD have also provided a link to their Transparency Portal if that would help you: Flock Safety - Normal IL PD Transparency Portal 
  
 
If you would like for The Town of Normal to proceed with your FOIA request, please provide clarifications within ten (10) days from the date of this email. Otherwise, The Town of Normal will consider your request withdrawn.

You may provide a response to The Town of Normal by any of the following methods:

1) by directly responding to this email.
2) by emailing deputyclerk@normalil.gov to clarify.
3) by regular mail to The Town of Normal 11 Uptown Circle, 2nd floor Normal, Illinois 61761.
4) in person at the Office of the Town Clerk of The Town of Normal 11 Uptown Circle, 2nd floor Normal, Illinois 61761. 
  
 Upon receipt of your clarification, The Town of Normal will further respond to your FOIA Request as required by law, including providing you with an estimate of costs associated with producing the requested records.

If you have any questions, please feel free to reply to this email communication. Thank you for your attention. 
  
 Thank you.

communications.communication:
A determination whether there are disclosable public records in the possession of this agency cannot be made within 10 days.  Up to 14 days additional time is required pursuant to Government Code section 7922.535(b) because the material to be checked is in other establishments separate from the office processing the request and because there is a need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request.  We will respond by April 6, 2026.

[cid:image001.png@01DCBAB6.95B4CAA0]

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: March 11 DA
communications.communication:
Thanks so much
Maxine Doogan

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: March 11 DA
communications.communication:
Ms. Doogan –

The following is in response to your requests made on March 11, 2026.

With regards to request #1, our office does not have a document or index that contains a list of the cases or documents you are specifically seeking.  The CPRA applies to existing records and does not require a public agency to create a record that does not exist.  (Gov’t Code §§ 7920.530, 7920.545.)  To determine this information would require a hand search of tens of thousands of files.  This would require us to search an enormous volume of data and is objectionable as unduly burdensome, something not required under the CPRA.  (See Gov’t Code § 7922.000; County of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 588, at 600-60; see also American Civil Liberties Union v. Deukmejian (1982) 32 Cal.3d 440, at 452-454.)

That being said, we are able to track cases filed from our office computerized case management system based on which specific criminal code sections are charged.  We can provide publicly available information from when we file a charging document, specifically, the docket number and the charges.  However, because we do not already have a report with the requested information, under the CPRA, the requester is responsible for paying the costs associated with preparing the computer inquiry to retrieve the data.  (Gov’t Code § 7922.575(b).)  It is estimated it will take one (1) hour of an IT Analyst II’s time at a cost of $103.81 to retrieve the lists of cases in which violations of the above noted Penal Code sections were charged for the time frame indicated.  You would be responsible for paying that cost before we proceed.  After the data is assembled, if it took less computer programming time than had been estimated, you would receive a refund; if it took more time, then you would be required to pay the balance before receiving the data.

As for the additional information you seek regarding the generated list of cases, that information is exempt from disclosure as part of our investigatory files and would not be provided.  Government Code section 7923.600(a) exempts from CPRA disclosure records that are part of investigatory files compiled by local agencies for law enforcement purposes, which includes district attorneys’ offices.  (See Rivero v. Superior Court (1997) 54 Cal.App.4th 1048; Rackaucas v. Superior Court (2012) 104 Cal.App.4th 169; Fagan v. Superior Court (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 607; County of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1099.)  This exemption applies not only to documents generated by the agency, but also to records gathered from other sources.  (See Haynie v. Superior Court (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1061.)  This exemption continues to apply even if the case is complete, and the investigation is closed.  (See Rivero v. Superior Court (1997) 54 Cal.App.4th 1048; Williams v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 337.)

If you wish to proceed with your request for a list of cases with the information we have indicated we can provide, please remit a check in the amount of $103.81 made out to the Sacramento County District Attorney’s Office.

With regards to requests #2 and #3, we can provide you now the attached documents.  However, additional time is required pursuant to Government Code section 7922.535(b) because the material to be checked is in other establishments separate from the office processing the request and because there is a need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request.  We will respond further on these requests by April 20, 2026.

Sincerely,
Michael Blazina


[cid:image001.png@01DCC5D7.A834DEF0]
Michael Blazina | Assistant District Attorney
Sacramento County District Attorney's Office
901 G Street | Sacramento, CA  95814

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: March 11 DA
communications.communication:
We do not have a contract between our office and the RESET Program.  This is a diversion program available from the court.  Additional information, including any program materials, would need to be requested from the Sacramento Superior Court, although some information is available on the court’s website at:
https://www.saccourt.ca.gov/criminal/collaborative-courts.aspx#reset

Conditions are available at:
https://www.saccourt.ca.gov/forms/docs/cr-reset-participant-conditions.pdf

title: FOIA Request: Flock Audits (Simpsonville Police Department)

communications.subject: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: FOIA Request: Flock Audits (Simpsonville Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2026 to April 30, 2026. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency. The report should include all selectable columns.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2026 to April 30, 2026. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. The report should include all selectable columns.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days for records less than 24-months-old, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

m

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: QNS emails (Queens Borough President's Office)
communications.communication:
This is an appeal of the above request. The Queens Borough President has constructively denied my request by failing to produce the records within its specified time frame and not providing a new deadline. This is unreasonable, given the narrow scope of the request.

Because of this appeal, QBP is required by law to provide specific reasons within 10 business days explaining why this request cannot be filled, or else, must provide the records sought.

According to the Committee on Open Government, if a government entity acknowledges that a request has been received, but has failed to grant access by the specific date given beyond twenty business days, or if the specific date given is unreasonable, a request may be considered to have been constructively denied (see §89[4][a]).  In such a circumstance, the denial may be appealed in accordance with §89(4)(a), which states in relevant part that:

“...any person denied access to a record may within thirty days appeal in writing such denial to the head, chief executive or governing body of the entity, or the person therefore designated by such head, chief executive, or governing body, who shall within ten business days of the receipt of such appeal fully explain in writing to the person requesting the record the reasons for further denial, or provide access to the record sought.” 

I, on behalf of New York Focus, am requesting either you fill this FOIL request, or else provide a full explanation as to the reason for the denial, as required by law, of this public record.

Sincerely,
Sam Mellins
Senior Reporter, New York Focus

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: QNS emails (Queens Borough President's Office)
communications.communication:
Mr. Mellins,

I serve as FOIL Appeals Officer for the Office of the Queens Borough President and am in receipt of your e-mail below.

We are currently in the process of fulfilling your request, and I want to be clear that your request has not been denied. While you characterized the scope of your request as narrow, I understand that the terms you provided produced approximately 5,500 potentially responsive e-mails. While I expect that the vast majority of those e-mails will in fact not be responsive, they still require manual review, which can be a time-consuming process.

As of now, our best estimate for when your request will be fulfilled is by February 24, 2022; we will contact you before that date if our office needs additional time to process your request. Please let me know if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Allan

Allan Swisher | General Counsel & Senior Policy Advisor
T 1.718.286.2881     E aswisher@queensbp.org<mailto:aswisher@queensbp.org>    W www.queensbp.org<https://gcc02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.queensbp.org%2F&data=05%7C01%7CQN08%40cb.nyc.gov%7Cc21af751a1604a8faff108da6bffad31%7C32f56fc75f814e22a95b15da66513bef%7C0%7C0%7C637941043231239659%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C3000%7C%7C%7C&sdata=hJfauypStXGGOIhDT1j7Bn5lOU0ufjMyw9%2FBJkOVNmQ%3D&reserved=0>
Queens Borough President Donovan Richards Jr. | 120-55 Queens Boulevard | Kew Gardens, NY 11424

communications.subject: FOIL Response
communications.communication:
Please find the attached records responsive to your FOIL requests dated October 24, 2022.

There are some redactions to these records. Specifically, pursuant to Freedom of Information Law Section 87(2)(g), portions of internal e-mails were redacted to withhold intra-agency communications, and e-mail attachments that were internal briefings or talking points were withheld in their entirety. Personal e-mail addresses of constituents who contacted our office were redacted pursuant to FOIL Section 87(2)(g).

You have the right to appeal this partial denial by responding to this e-mail within 30 days and providing the reason for your appeal.

Allan Swisher | General Counsel & Senior Policy Advisor
T 1.718.286.2881     E alswisher@queensbp.nyc.gov<mailto:alswisher@queensbp.nyc.gov>
Queens Borough President Donovan Richards Jr. | 120-55 Queens Boulevard | Kew Gardens, NY 11424

files.title: Innovation QNS FOIL Request
files.source: Queens Borough President's Office, New York City, NY

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Emails (Queens Borough President's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Emails exchanged since July 1, 2022, with Councilmember Julie Won, or her staff, including email addresses CGil@council.nyc.gov, IBlasenstein@council.nyc.gov, ncorona@council.nyc.gov, JLaing@council.nyc.gov, or district26@council.nyc.gov.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sam Mellins

communications.subject: FOIL Response
communications.communication:
Please find the attached records responsive to your FOIL requests dated October 24, 2022.

There are some redactions to these records. Specifically, pursuant to Freedom of Information Law Section 87(2)(g), portions of internal e-mails were redacted to withhold intra-agency communications, and e-mail attachments that were internal briefings or talking points were withheld in their entirety. Personal e-mail addresses of constituents who contacted our office were redacted pursuant to FOIL Section 87(2)(g).

You have the right to appeal this partial denial by responding to this e-mail within 30 days and providing the reason for your appeal.

Allan Swisher | General Counsel & Senior Policy Advisor
T 1.718.286.2881     E alswisher@queensbp.nyc.gov<mailto:alswisher@queensbp.nyc.gov>
Queens Borough President Donovan Richards Jr. | 120-55 Queens Boulevard | Kew Gardens, NY 11424

files.title: Innovation QNS FOIL Request
files.source: Queens Borough President's Office, New York City, NY

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (City Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by the chair of the select board related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal and secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (City Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following New Hampshire Right to Know Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on March 23, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Lebanon, NH: 91-A request
communications.communication:
Good Evening Audrey and Jonathan,
We have received your 91-A request for the Mayor's email communications between January 1, 2026, and January 31, 2026.
I will coordinate with the relevant department heads to gather this information. In accordance with our policy, any available documentation will be provided electronically on a USB drive, with a fee of $25.00 for the device. The device must be picked up. Please confirm if you can pick up the device. This information is critical to have before we start researching our records. Should you provide confirmation, the device will be ready on Monday, April 13.
Please let me know if you have any questions.
Jaseya Ewing, CMC

title: Fort Worth ChatGPT | Tarrant County Commissioner's Court

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Fort Worth ChatGPT | Tarrant County Commissioner's Court
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Chat histories of all ChatGPT sessions conducted by county employees on government-owned devices or used in job-related functions in the following departments: Office of the Tarrant County Judge and Tarrant County Commissioners' Court (including Precincts's 1,2,3,4)

The timeframe for this request is 4/1/2025 to the date this request is processed. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Please make records available in installments as they are ready to release.

If it’s helpful, please share with respondents the following help article and instructions for exporting ChatGPT histories into a .zip folder:

https://help.openai.com/en/articles/7260999-how-do-i-export-my-chatgpt-history-and-data

1. Sign in to ChatGPT.
2. In the top right corner of the page click on your profile icon.
3. Click Settings.
4. Click Data Controls menu.
5. Under Export Data click Export.
6. In the confirmation screen click Confirm export.
7. You should receive an email with your data.
Note: The link in the email expires after 24 hours
8. Click Download data export to download a .zip file will be downloaded including your chat history in chat.html as well as your other data on file with us

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bradford William Davis

communications.subject: Tarrant County District Attorney's Office - District Attorney's Office Public Information Act Request CDA-2025-2289 - Notice of Costs
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor Bradford William Davis, 
  
 On 10/28/2025 the undersigned received your submitted request under the Public Information Act (PIA) seeking information maintained by the Tarrant County District Attorney’s Office. 
  
 Please be advised that there are costs involved in fulfilling your PIA request. The PIA authorizes the District Attorney’s Office to charge $.10 per page, $15.00 per hour for personnel costs associated with fulfilling a request, 20% of personnel costs for overhead, $10.00 per recording of body worn camera, and $1.00 per full minute of body worn camera or audio footage. Other costs may apply depending on what is being requested. These cost rules are set out in Title 1, Texas Administrative Code, Chapter 70. 
  
 If you are still interested in obtaining the information in possession of the District Attorney’s Office, please see the attached Written Itemized Statement of Costs. 
  
 The attached is an estimate only. If the estimated cost is more than the actual cost incurred by the District Attorney’s Office, you will be refunded accordingly. There may be less costly methods of viewing the records. Please feel free to contact the office anytime if you wish to discuss alternative methods; or, if you would like to discuss how the Criminal District Attorney’s Office may lower its cost estimate; or if you believe that the District Attorney’s Office has made an error or misunderstood your request, please give us a call. 
  
 If the District Attorney’s Office later determines, before it makes the copy or the paper record available, that the estimated charges will exceed the charges detailed in their attached itemized cost estimate by twenty (20) percent or more, the District Attorney’s Office will send you an updated statement that details all estimated charges that will be imposed, including any allowable charges for labor or personnel costs as required by Section 552.2615(b) Texas Government Code. Further, if no responsive records are located your payment will be refunded. 
  
 Please respond to this itemized cost estimate from the District Attorney’s Office within ten (10) business days and include the following information: 
  
 (1) your choice of your mailing, facsimile transmission or electronic mail address; and 
  
 (2) that you either accept the estimated charges; or you are modifying your request; or, you have sent a complaint to the Texas Attorney General alleging that you are being overcharged. See Section 552.2615(a)(3) and (b) Texas Government Code. 
  
 If you do not respond in writing within ten (10) business days from the date of this itemized cost estimate as outlined, your request will be considered automatically withdrawn, Section 552.2615(b) Texas Gov Code. 
  
 For your convenience, complaints regarding overcharges may be made to the following: 
 Office of the Attorney General 
 Cost Hotline at (512) 475-2497 
 Toll-Free at 1-888-672-6787 
  
 If you agree to the charges set forth herein, please forward your firm check, cashier’s check or money order payable and delivered to as follows: 
  
 $108.00 payable to the 
  
 Tarrant County District Attorney’s Office 
 Attn: Civil Division - Open Records 
 401 W. Belknap St, 9th Floor 
 Fort Worth, TX 76196 
  
 If paying in cash, exact change must be submitted. Debit or credit card payments are not accepted. 
  
 Your written response to this itemized cost estimate may be sent by regular U.S. mail, electronic mail, and facsimile or in person through any of the following points of contact: 
  
 Paul Nguyen, Assistant District Attorney 
 Tarrant County District Attorney’s Office 
 Tim Curry Criminal Justice Center, Civil Division, 9th Floor 
 401 West Belknap 
 Fort Worth, Texas 76196-0201 
 Facsimile (817)884-1675 
 E-Mail – pknguyen@tarrantcountytx.gov 
 E-MAIL- OpenRecords@tarrantcountytx.gov 
  
 If you have any questions or comments concerning the attached itemized cost estimate from the District Attorney’s Office, please call me at 817.884.1233. 
  
 Attachment: Written Itemized Statement of Costs 
  
 _______________________________________________________________________________________________________
Powered by

title: FOIA Request – Police Funding & Surveillance Spending

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request – Police Funding & Surveillance Spending
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request copies of the following records related to the Flint Police Department:

Surveillance & Data Collection
 - The locations of all Flock Cameras and any associated data retention policies.
 - Any purchase orders, contracts, or policies regarding License Plate Readers (LPRs).
 - Copies of all records related to facial recognition technology, including vendors, contracts, and policies.
 - All records related to the use of social media monitoring tools, AI-powered online tracking, or digital surveillance of citizens.
 - Any records regarding Stingray (cell-site simulator) technology, including deployment logs, policies, and training materials.
 - All gunshot detection system (ShotSpotter) reports, including statistics on false positives and policies on police responses.
 - Any data-sharing agreements between Flint PD and state/federal agencies, including local Fusion Centers.

Crowd Control & Protest Surveillance
 - Records of drone surveillance at protests, public events, or general policing over the last five years.
 - Deployment logs for crowd control equipment (tear gas, rubber bullets, LRADs, etc.).
 - Any internal memos, training materials, or policies regarding identifying and disrupting protest organizers.

Budget & Equipment Acquisition
 - A full breakdown of grants, federal funding, and private donations received for police surveillance and riot control equipment.
 - A report on how funds from civil asset forfeiture have been spent in the last five years.
 - Any contracts with private surveillance companies (e.g., Palantir, Flock Safety, Clearview AI, Axon).


I request digital copies of these documents. If any part of this request is denied, please provide the specific legal exemption being cited.

I request a fee waiver as this information is in the public interest.

Please confirm receipt of this request and respond within 5 business days, as required by Michigan FOIA law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michigan Transparency

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Dear Keweenaw County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Keweenaw County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Hi Julie,

Thanks for your April 20, 2026 written response. As I understand your response to be a denial of my FOIA request — since the responsive records are being conditioned on a $1,250 fee not authorized under FOIA — please treat this message as a written appeal under MCL 15.240.

Three things to flag.

1. "Act or statute" in MCL 15.234(10) means a state statute that explicitly authorizes the sale or sets the fee. The Michigan Court of Appeals construed this carve-out in Grebner v. Clinton Charter Twp., 216 Mich. App. 736 (1996):

"A primary definition of the word 'specific' is 'explicit.' … In contrast, the Michigan Election Law, § 522, provides only for the payment of costs of preparing copies of voter registration records, as opposed to their sale. Clearly, this is not explicit authorization of the sale of voter registration rolls."

216 Mich. App. at 743.

MCL 48.101 governs transcripts from the tax roll maintained by the Treasurer's office. It does not explicitly authorize the sale of the Equalization Department's assessment roll, and it does not set a specific fee for such records. The $1,250 figure is a departmental/BoC fee-schedule number, not a fee "specifically provided by an act or statute" within the meaning of MCL 15.234(10).

EAPRA (MCL 15.441–15.446) fails the same test: it doesn't explicitly authorize the sale of assessment rolls, and it doesn't set a specific fee.

2. EAPRA has its own savings clause. MCL 15.443(4): "This act does not limit the right of a person to inspect or copy a public record under [FOIA]." The Senate Fiscal Agency's committee summary for the enacting bill (HB 5832, PA 462 of 1996) puts it plainly:

"The bill would not limit access to a public record under the FOIA. A public record available by enhanced access would have to be made available for inspection or copying in accordance with [FOIA]."

SFA Committee Summary HB 5832 (H-3), 9-18-96, at 2. (Attached.)

3. MI's FOIA has a clear list of exemptions under MCL 15.243. The records I requested are not listed there. FOIA (1976 PA 442) predates EAPRA (1996 PA 462) by ~20 years — if the Legislature had intended EAPRA or MCL 48.101 to make assessment records FOIA-exempt, they would have added them to 15.243.

Side point on custody. I am not requesting anything from the Treasurer's office — no tax billing records, no delinquent tax rolls, no payment receipts, no forfeiture records. I am requesting the assessment roll, which is maintained by the Equalization Department under MCL 211.34 / 211.34d. The assessment roll records valuations (SEV, taxable, classification, PRE). The Treasurer's tax roll records collection. Distinct records, distinct custodians — MCL 48.101 speaks to the Treasurer's records, not Equalization's. I also never requested GIS data.

Requested relief. Pursuant to MCL 15.240(2), I respectfully ask that you:

-- Reverse the denial; and
-- Produce the assessment roll at FOIA cost itemized under MCL 15.234 on the County's cost itemization form.
If this appeal is denied in whole or in part, I reserve all rights under MCL 15.240(1)(b), including a civil action in Keweenaw County Circuit Court with fee-shifting under MCL 15.240(6) and punitive damages under MCL 15.240(7) if the denial is found arbitrary and capricious.

Thanks,
Alexander Munro
awmunro@gmail.com · 734-272-6612
34 N Huron St, OFC
Ypsilanti, MI 48197

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Hi Julie,

Could you open a new request for me under the FOIA? This would be for a distinct category of records, for a distinct type of record from the tax rolls I was denied previously.

Could you please send me:
-Any correspondence (letters, emails) b/t Keweenaw County and Zillow (or Zillow, Inc; Zillow Group, etc), from the year 2010 thru current
-Any agreements (licenses, MOUs), invoices or contracts with Zillow or other data brokers who have purchased data sales like the $1250 fee quoted previously, from 2010 thru current
-Same as above (Correspondence, emails,q invoices & contracts) for ATTOM, Remine, Black Knight, First American  (aka Cotality/Corelogic) and any other data brokers who have purchased copies of Keweenaw property tax records at a cost above what's authorized under the foia, from 2010 thru current
-Same as above for BS&A software (the software suite which I believe the tax records are kept in after they come from the Board of Review), from 2010 thru current

If any of the above records do not exist, please confirm in writing per MCL15.235

I would appreciate a heads up if the cost for this new request exceeds $75 -- if it's less than that, I'll send a check.

Thank you,
Alexander Munro, 34 N Huron St OFC, Ypsilanti MI 48197

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: LAPD 1033/1208 Program Transfer Records (1996–1998)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I respectfully request copies of the following records maintained by the Defense Logistics Agency, DLA Disposition Services (formerly the Defense Reutilization and Marketing Service, DRMS/DRMO), and any predecessor offices that administered the 1208 Program and its successor 1033 Program:

1/ The complete application(s), Memorandum of Agreement, State Plan of Operation, and any signed participation agreements submitted by or on behalf of the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) to participate in the 1208 Program (10 U.S.C. § 2576, Pub. L. 101-189) and/or the 1033 Program (10 U.S.C. § 2576a, Pub. L. 104-201) between January 1, 1996 and December 31, 1998.
2/ All transfer records, requisition forms (including DD Form 1348-1A or equivalent), shipping documents, property accountability records, and inventory lists documenting the transfer of M16 rifles (NSN 1005-00-073-9421 or related national stock numbers) from any Department of Defense source to LAPD during that period — including but not limited to the approximately 600 M16 rifles reported to have been transferred to LAPD following the February 28, 1997 North Hollywood bank shootout.
3/ Records identifying the originating military installation(s) for the transferred rifles, including any documentation showing shipment from the Anniston Army Depot (Anniston, Alabama) or other Army depots.
4/ Any correspondence, memoranda, or email between DLA/DRMS/DRMO personnel and LAPD, the California State Coordinator for the 1033 Program, or the U.S. Army concerning the LAPD rifle transfer(s) during the above period.

Format: Electronic copies (PDF) via email are preferred.

Fee waiver: I request a fee waiver under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii) because disclosure is in the public interest, concerning the operations of government, specifically the militarization of civilian policing and the federal transfer of military weapons to local law enforcement. This request is not for commercial use. In the alternative, please notify me before incurring fees exceeding $25.

Expedited processing: Not requested.

Please segregate and release any non-exempt portions of responsive records and cite the specific exemption(s) relied upon for any withholding. If responsive records are maintained by another DoD component (e.g., U.S. Army TACOM, Anniston Army Depot, or the Army's Law Enforcement Support Office), please refer this request accordingly and notify me of the referral.

Sincerely,
Daniel Brown

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: 321D-IR-A7788
communications.communication:
Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on "Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request" on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@ic.fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 134 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 523 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,335 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 1,875 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
Record/Information Dissemination Section (RIDS) FBI-Information Management Division
170 Marcel Drive, Winchester, VA 22602-4843
Direct: (540) 868-4593
Fax: (540) 868-4391
Questions E-mail: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 25-310
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All active contracts, NDAs, MOAs, MOUs, invoices, and other agreements with Flock Safety.
2. The administrative tracking file for request PDR 25-310
3. All emails, text messages, chat messages (Teams, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, etc), and/or call logs regarding the fulfillment of PDR 25-310, including accompanying metadata. For any responsive chat and text messages, please provide messages sent/received in that thread within 1 hour of the responsive message as context.
4. All emails sent to and received from @flocksafety.com email addresses between 11/7/2025 and the date this request was processed, including attachments and associated metadata.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

Thank you,
Rose

communications.subject: [Document Released to Requester] City of Prosser, WA public records request #25-334
communications.communication:
City of Prosser, WA ************************************************************************ Documents have been released for record request #25-334 along with the following message: Thank you for your request for public records. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, the City of Prosser has received your request. The third installment of documents the City believes are responsive to your request are available in the request portal. Please be aware that the City searched its records only. The databank we searched does not account for any other agency, community, or police department. You will find that there are black boxes (redactions) on various pages of the responsive documents. The black box may be small or large depending on the amount of information that is redacted. There may also be multiple redactions on one page. Within the redaction is a white code. (For example, 1.1, 2.1, 2.2, etc.) That code designates the statutory exemption applicable to the redacted information. Provided with these records is a key to the codes. This key identifies the statutory basis for the exemption as well as a brief explanation for why the exemption applies to the redacted information. As you review these responsive documents, you can refer to the key to identify the exemption. The City has determined it needs an additional 14 business days in order to locate and assemble additional documents that may potentially be responsive to your request. You will be notified no later than March 24, 2026 as to the status of your request. communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Tarantula File, SY26
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the tarantula file.

Please provide this file for SY 2025-2026. This is the file that shows whether a school has an attendance boundary, how many students attend the school, how many students reside in the boundary, how many reside in the boundary and attend the school, how many attend but don't reside, how many reside but don't attend, , etc.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

communications.subject: FOIA Request :: N019623-051826
communications.communication:
Dear Miriam : 
 
Thank you for your interest in Chicago Public Schools. Your FOIA request has been submitted. Your FOIA Center reference number for tracking purposes is: N019623-051826 
You have requested the following records: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

I am requesting a copy of the tarantula file. 

Please provide this file for SY 2025-2026. This is the file that shows whether a school has an attendance boundary, how many students attend the school, how many students reside in the boundary, how many reside in the boundary and attend the school, how many attend but don't reside, how many reside but don't attend, , etc. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2292936/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19623

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Asian Giant Hornets (Washington State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Widespread news reports and agency releases show that there has been the spread and distribution of "Asian Giant Hornets" / "Murder Hornets" in the last year. Earlier this year, a joint press conference was held about eradication efforts: https://agr.wa.gov/about-wsda/news-and-media-relations/news-releases?article=32279

Therefore, I request the following information:

- Any lab reports, internal communications, analyses, warnings, or other guidances about the Asian Giant Hornets.
- Any case reports about Asian Giant Hornet sightings or suspected sightings
 - Any sightings / emails / audio recordings / photos / videos from the public about Asian Giant Hornets sent through "agr.wa.gov/hornets, via email at hornets@agr.wa.gov, or by calling 1-800-443-6684" 
- Any forensic analysis or communication with law enforcement (state, local, or national) about Asian Giant Hornets.
- Any presentations or other documents prepared about Asian Giant Hornets
- Any photos or video related to Asian Giant Hornets

Please search from May 1, 2020 until the day this request is processed.

This request is being made for use on Motherboard, VICE Media's science and technology website. It is not for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Koebler

title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Clara County Registrar of Voters)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Clara County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in your County in that year. In case it is helpful to see an example of the type of data I am requesting, Alameda County has these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

communications.subject: CPRA: Aleksandra Conevska - 3/2/26
communications.communication:
Dear Aleksandra Conevska,


The Registrar of Voters’ Office has performed a diligent search in order to locate and produce the records that you requested, and we are providing a partial response today. The responsive records may be accessed here: [​Folder icon]  Aleksandra Conevska Nov 4 2024 CVRs<https://sccconnect-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/christina_rivaslouie_rov_sccgov_org/IgCs-Muy7ZFiR6kyY2gBZLqfAWSWRyvhgNO2sZaBwcW03TY?email=205375-13067991%40requests.muckrock.com&e=ldOhvA>. Please note, this link will expire in 30 days – download the files accordingly.  When prompted, each file may be viewed in the “Read Only” mode. We further note that certain information in the records you requested is exempt from disclosure and was redacted for reasons of personal privacy (Cal. Const. art. I, sec. I; Cal. Govt. Code §§ 7924.005, 7927.705, 7924.000; Cal. Evid. Code § 1040; City of San Jose v. Superior Court Santa Clara County (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 1008). We will be providing additional materials on a rolling basis going forward as the records are searched for, collected and appropriately examined for appropriate exemptions.  We anticipate providing you with the next batch of responsive records within one month from the date of this email.



Thank you.



[cid:image004.png@01DCA590.8EF58140]



Policies & Procedures Division

County of Santa Clara – Registrar of Voters



1555 Berger Drive, Bldg. 2

San Jose, CA 95112

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Clara County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
Hi,

Thank you for your prompt and helpful response to my request. I was able to download the read-only CVR files that correspond to the 2024 General Election. 

I understand you anticipate providing me with an additional batch of records and that this was only a partial response to my request, and I really appreciate you taking the time to locate and properly process the relevant records. I know you need more time but I'm hoping that in the meantime, you can clarify two things about the coming additional records you plan to try to provide to complete your response to my request. This will help for my own planning. 

First, could you clarify whether in the next batch of responsive records, you plan to try to provide them in a usable data format, rather than read-only? I understand you wanted to provide a quick partial response to my request and that the non-read only, usable redacted records, may take longer to process. But could you confirm whether you plan to try to provide the non-read only records where possible? I do not believe that read-only files comply with my request (at least for 2024 as the mandatory retention period has not yet expired) unless there is a technical reason why, and I'm happy to submit a technical note as to why that is. 

Second, could you clarify whether you are trying to locate the 2022 records, or if these have already been purged given the data retention requirements? I just want to know for my own planning if Santa Clara County no longer has the 2022 records at all, which I would understand as we are passed the mandatory record retention period. 

Apologies for the long message. I really appreciate your assistance on this, and all of the work your office does.

All my best,
Aleksandra

 The public information request for Request Number: PIR-2026-1051 has been completed.  Please click on the request link to download the response.

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Positive drug screenings by facility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A database or spreadsheet of the total number of positive drug screenings by facility at the time of this request and for the last five years. The West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation Jail Units Inmate Handbook (https://wvpolicy.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Jail-Handbook-All-Jails-2024.04.08.pdf) describes that each inmate is screened. I am not requesting the individual-level data, but aggregate data by facility. I request this data in a machine-readable database or spreadsheet format such as Excel, CSV, Access or SQL.

I also request all documentation that describes the layout of the database’s tables and fields, and that provides the definitions of any field names, codes, and abbreviations used in the database. This information may be gathered together in a single document like a data dictionary, entity layout, or user's guide. If that is the case, I request access to and copy of said document.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following West Virginia Freedom of Information Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on May 12, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Washington Secretary of State public records request #26-180
communications.communication:
Washington Secretary of State ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-180: Audrey: Thank you for your patience as we review your public records request with the Office of the Secretary of State. We will contact you on or before February 27, 2026 to provide either (i) any responsive records OSOS has located in that time or (ii) a revised estimate of when responsive records will be available or (iii) that there are no records responsive to your request. Please note that our estimate is based on multiple factors such as: (a) number of public records requests OSOS has open (b) the complexity of those requests (c) the complexity of the request you submitted (d) staff and other resources available to respond to public records requests. Additional dates may be needed to provide further batches of documents depending on the volume of the responsive records pertaining to your request. If you submitted a request that contains multiple parts and there are parts you wish OSOS to prioritize, please respond to this acknowledgment email so we are aware of what to prioritize for release. Please use the above Request ID# to see the status of your request by signing into the Washington Secretary of State portal.  Thank you, Public Records Office ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Washington Secretary of State.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Catawba County Sheriff)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Catawba County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to Cowboy Concealments or Cowboy Streetlight Concealments dated between 1/1/2024 and 5/21/2026. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.
