title: Wirt County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Wirt County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kierstyn Aubrecht
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 20, 2026

19 Washington St, Elizabeth, WV 26143                                                                                              

To Whom it may concern,
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Wirt County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 

The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely,
Kierstyn Aubrecht
kra00019@mix.wvu.edu

disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable
nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires. I request
that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor
at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public
interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please
contact me if you have any questions about my media status. The requested
documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is
not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I
would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of
fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by
e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your
anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your
response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.
Sincerely, Matthew Petti Upload documents directly:
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2138324/

**

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Army OMPF: Edward Philip Cutolo
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) concerning: 'Colonel Edward Philip Cutolo'.

Born : 04 Nov 1931 Manhattan, New York, USA
Died: 26 May 1980 Sculthorpe, Norfolk, United Kingdom

Branch: United States Army
Years of Service: 1954 to 1980

United States Military Academy Class of 1954
Cullum No. 20153

Please include the the following in addition to the OMPF:

1. Dates of service, dates of ranks, stations, list of awards
2. Current and previous assignment forms
3. Section II military education/training courses
4. Section V (Miscellaneous) form
5. Copies of commendations and award citations
6 . Any other releasable material such performance reports, discharge papers etc..

https://www.vlm.cem.va.gov/EDWARDPCUTOLO/75420
https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/183591851/edward-philip-cutolo
https://valor.militarytimes.com/recipient/recipient-145721/

PLEASE NOTE: I am requesting hard copies of the requested material.

Please scan and email PDF copies to: requests@muckrock.com

or mail hard copies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON MA 
02115-4577

In order to help you determine my status for the purpose of assessing fees, I am seeking information for historical research and not for any commercial benefit.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joseph Lloyd

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received president/chair of the board of alderman related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: RE: [External Email] New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)
communications.communication:
Please be advised that the City of Manchester is in receipt of your request for records below. The information you have requested is outside the control of this office and maintained by other departments to the extent that it exists.  We have requested our Information Systems Department to process your request as soon as possible.

The chairman of the Board of Mayor & Aldermen officially takes the position when appointed by their colleagues at the first organizational meeting of the board in January in even years, in this case January 6, 2026.  We will request chairman emails between 1/6/26 – 1/31/26 per the intent of your request.

A copy of your request and this communication has been sent to the aforementioned City representative.   You will be contacted when your request has been completed and arrangements can be made to view any related information.  I will provide you with an estimated time of completion as soon as I receive a response from the IT Department.

Matthew Normand
City Clerk
Office of the City Clerk
One City Hall Plaza
Manchester, NH 03101
T:(603) 792-6630 F:(603) 624-6481
www.manchesternh.gov/cityclerk<http://www.manchesternh.gov/cityclerk>

communications.subject: [External Message Added] County of Marin, CA public records request #26-308
communications.communication:
County of Marin, CA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-308: Dear Requester, The County has reviewed your request and identified responsive records. The County will produce pictures/photographs of property seized from Binford Road that are not otherwise exempt from disclosure under the California Public Records Act (CPRA). The County requires additional time to collect and review these records. We anticipate producing the records on a rolling basis with an initial production on or before April 24, 2026.  The County is withholding certain responsive records pursuant to applicable exemptions under the CPRA. The applicable exemptions include: * * Records protected by the attorney-client privilege or attorney work product doctrine. Government Code Section 7927.705 and Evidence Code Section 950 et seq. * Records where the public interest in withholding the records clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure. Government Code Section 7922.000. * Personnel, medical or similar files, the disclosure of which would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. See Government Code Section 7927.700. * Records, the disclosure of which are exempted or prohibited pursuant to federal or state law. Government Code Section 7927.705. Thank you. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at County of Marin, CA.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Custer, Email Log - CPS
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. A log of all emails to/from the Jennifer Custer, from April 1, 2026 to April 30, 2026.

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

communications.subject: Welcome to the FOIA Center!
communications.communication:
Thank you for registering with the CPS FOIA Center. Please log in to the FOIA Center to update any contact or password information and to track the progress of your request. 
 Your Login ID is:  211203-83588345@requests.muckrock.com If you did not submit your request through the Records Center but are receiving this e-mail, an account has been created for you where you can track and monitor the status of your request, and receive any documents in response to your request. If you have never used this system or cannot remember your password you may request a temporary password here: Request Temporary Password. (https://cps.mycusthelp.com/webapp/_rs/forgotpassword.aspx?) 
 
 
 
This is an auto-generated email and has originated from an unmonitored email account. Please DO NOT REPLY.

communications.subject: FOIA Request :: N019586-051326
communications.communication:
Dear Miriam : 
 
Thank you for your interest in Chicago Public Schools. Your FOIA request has been submitted. Your FOIA Center reference number for tracking purposes is: N019586-051326 
You have requested the following records: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. A log of all emails to/from the Jennifer Custer, from April 1, 2026 to April 30, 2026. 

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2289329/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19586

05/28/2026 Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019586-051326 Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. A log of all emails to/from the Jennifer Custer, from April 1, 2026 to April 30, 2026. 

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2289329/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. Student/family information has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Section 7.5(h) exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (McKinley County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (McKinley County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

If not, the standard fee for a USB flash drive or CD-ROM would be $5.00.

Respectfully,

James Maiorano III
Sheriff, McKinley County

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19587

communications.subject: Your City of Sheboygan, WI public records request #26-519 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
City of Sheboygan, WI ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-519 has been submitted successfully. Thank you for submitting your public records request. The City of Sheboygan will provide the requested information as promptly as possible. Please note that requests are processed in the order they are received, and response times may vary depending on the complexity of the request and the volume of pending requests. If any fees apply, we will notify you before proceeding. The City of Sheboygan is committed to fulfilling all requests in accordance with Wisconsin’s open records laws while ensuring transparency and accessibility. Please be aware that communications between City staff and the City Attorney are protected as attorney-client privileged and will not be released. Additionally, some records may require additional time for review or redaction to comply with legal and privacy requirements. If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to send us a message through this portal. We’re here to help! The due date for your request may be later than usual because the office is closed on: * May 25: Memorial Day As the requester, you can always see the status of your request by signing into the City of Sheboygan, WI portal. If you haven't already signed in, you may need to activate or setup your account to get started. Once your account is activated, you can communicate directly with the City of Sheboygan, WI through NextRequest. ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact City of Sheboygan, WI.

Change your email settings | Visit our help center

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Georgia Power
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act (O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq.), the City of Atlanta Department of Procurement acknowledges receipt of your request submitted via email on Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at 8:52 AM. Please accept this as the Department’s statutorily required response.


In your request of May 27, 2026, you asked for the following:


A copy of purchase order number 52524873, dated 6/26/2025 from Georgia Power for $6,120.- Attached

A copy of all agreements with Georgia Power related to purchase order number 52524873.- Please be advised that DOP has no responsive agreement or contract document related to your request for Georgia Power. The procurement was processed as a small purchase in accordance with the City of Atlanta Procurement Code. Under the City’s procurement regulations, a small purchase is a procurement made for goods or services that falls within the monetary threshold established for streamlined purchasing procedures and does not require a formal written contract or competitive sealed solicitation process. As such, no formal agreement was executed for this purchase.

Accordingly, there are no additional responsive documents in DOP’s custody and control related to a contract or agreement for this procurement.

Please let us know if you have any additional questions.

title: FBI Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

FOIA Request: Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration

To the FBI FOIA/PA Division:

Under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), I am requesting access to specific administrative, statistical, and procurement records pertaining to the operations of the Pensacola Resident Agency (RA), which operates under the jurisdiction of the Jacksonville Field Office, focusing on activities within Escambia County, Florida.

To avoid undue burden and ensure a precise search, this request does not seek raw intelligence, personally identifiable information (PII), or active investigative files. Instead, it seeks the following high-level administrative and statistical records generated between October 1, 2022, and the present date:

1. Statistical Accomplishment and Case Metrics
A statistical breakdown or summary report of the number of investigative cases opened, closed, or currently active at the Pensacola RA, categorized by FBI Classification Code or programmatic area. Specifically, I am requesting the raw case count numbers for operations falling under:

Counterintelligence (CI)

Counterterrorism (CT)

Cyber Crime

Violent Crime / Safe Streets

Public Corruption

2. Watchlisting and Task Force Administration

A copy of all active Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), Joint Task Force agreements, or operational charters between the FBI (Jacksonville FO / Pensacola RA) and the Escambia County Sheriff’s Office (ECSO) and/or the Pensacola Police Department (PPD).

Any localized administrative guidelines, memorandums, or statistical reports regarding the management, nomination, or auditing of the localized Terrorist Screening Database (TSDB) / "threat watchlisting" protocols in conjunction with Escambia County local law enforcement partners.

3. Procurement, Inventory, and Budgetary Allocation

Copies of localized procurement logs, purchase orders, or budgetary allocations specifically designated for the Pensacola RA.

Vendor contracts or equipment leases originating from or executed for the Pensacola RA, specifically pertaining to IT administration, network infrastructure, office space leases, and surveillance/telecommunications hardware.

Fee Waiver Request:
I request a waiver of all fees for this request. Disclosure of the requested information to me is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government—specifically, how federal law enforcement resources, budget allocations, and inter-agency task forces are being utilized in the Escambia County region. This request is not being made for commercial purposes.

If any portion of this request is denied or heavily redacted, please provide a detailed justification citing the specific FOIA exemptions applied. If the search for any specific item is deemed too broad, please process the remaining items and contact me to refine the scope of the delayed item.

Thank you for your lawful compliance and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1732075-000
communications.communication:
To the Public Information Officer:

I am in receipt of your letter dated April 17, 2026, regarding FOIPA Request No. 1732483-000 (Statistical Accomplishment and Case Metrics for the Pensacola RA), which denied my request for a public interest fee waiver.

In order to prevent this request from being administratively closed and to ensure processing continues immediately, I hereby agree to pay standard processing fees up to $25.00.

I reserve my right to appeal any final fee assessment. Furthermore, as I have been categorized as a "general (all others) requester," I respectfully remind the Bureau that under standard FOIA fee regulations [5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iv)(II)], I am entitled to the first two (2) hours of search time and the first one hundred (100) pages of duplication completely free of charge before any billable fees are incurred.

Given that statistical summaries are generally brief, I expect the responsive records to fall well within this free threshold.

Please consider this my formal authorization to proceed. I look forward to the release of these records.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1732075-000
communications.communication:
To the FBI FOIA/PA Division / Information Management Division:

RE: Administrative Fee Stipulations for FOIPA Request Nos. 1732075-000 and 1732483-000

I am in receipt of your correspondence dated April 17, 2026 (regarding Request No. 1732483-000) and April 28, 2026 (regarding Request No. 1732075-000). To ensure the administrative files for both tracking numbers are complete and processing is not delayed, please note the following directives for the official record:

Regarding FOIPA Request No. 1732483-000 (Statistical & Case Metrics):
While I disagree with the assessment, I will not appeal the public interest fee waiver denial at this time in order to expedite processing. As an "All Other" requester, I hereby invoke my statutory entitlement to two (2) free hours of search time and 100 pages of free duplication. Please proceed with the search using this standard allowance.

Regarding FOIPA Request No. 1732075-000 (Inter-Agency MOUs):
I acknowledge the "unusual circumstances" extension applied to this file. I am reiterating my request for a public interest fee waiver for this specific tracking number, as the disclosure of executed inter-agency agreements significantly contributes to the public's understanding of joint federal/local law enforcement operations.

Global Fee Cap Stipulation (Applicable to Both Requests):
For both of the above-referenced requests, if the processing costs exhaust the statutory free search time and duplication allowances, I stipulate a hard maximum fee cap of $25.00 per request.

Do not exceed $25.00 on either file. If the estimated fees to process either request exceed this cap, you are instructed to pause processing on that specific file and provide me with a detailed, itemized fee estimate so that I may narrow the scope accordingly.

Please update both administrative files and proceed with the searches.

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: PD Settlement Agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I am resubmitting this request originally forwarded to the city by the Elizabeth Reilly at the Nashua Police Department in January. Would you be able to assist with these records? Please let me know, and thank you for your help. 

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

 A copy of every agreement to settle a lawsuit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim against the police department over the past 10 years. This covers all agreements that would fall under New Hampshire RSA 91-A:4 (Right-to-Know Law), VI:

VI. Every agreement to settle a lawsuit against a governmental unit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim, entered into by any political subdivision or its insurer, shall be kept on file at the municipal clerk's office and made available for public inspection for a period of no less than 10 years from the date of settlement.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

City of Nashua, NH ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-372: RSA 91-A if a public body or agency is unable to make a governmental record available for immediate inspection and copying the public body or agency shall, within 5 business days of a request. Provide a written statement of the time reasonably necessary to determine whether the request shall be granted or denied and the reason for the delay. (RSA 91-A:4, IV (b), (3). 91-A:4 Minutes and Records Available for Public Inspection. I. Every citizen during the regular or business hours of all public bodies or agencies, and on the regular business premises of such public bodies or agencies, has the right to inspect all governmental records in the possession, custody, or control of such public bodies or agencies, including minutes of meetings of the public bodies, and to copy and make memoranda or abstracts of the records or minutes so inspected, except as otherwise prohibited by statute or RSA 91-A:5. In this section, " to copy " means the reproduction of original records by whatever method, including but not limited to photography, photostatic copy, printing, or electronic or tape recording. All settlement agreements are held in the City Clerk's Office. The requested records will be available for inspection as in accordance with RSA 91-A:4, I, the City of Nashua’s City Hall is open Monday through Friday 8am to 5pm with an availability to inspect governmental records. Upon, your review and inspection of the records sought in your communication as dated and cited above, these requests will be considered satisfied. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Follow-Up FOIL Request: Flock Safety contract authorization, site selection, and cancellation records
communications.communication:
Dear Supervisor Walsh,

I am appealing the denial of my April 15, 2026 FOIL request, denied by Town Clerk Madelin Dafoe on April 17, 2026, on the ground that the Town of Pine Plains “does not maintain or possess the records” requested.

I respectfully request reversal of that denial.

The basis for my appeal is straightforward. Records previously produced concerning the Pine Plains Flock Safety matter already show responsive communications involving Town officials and offices, including communications involving you and the supervisor’s office. Those records indicate that at least some responsive records were maintained by, sent to, received by, or used by Town personnel in connection with Town business.

My follow-up request specifically sought additional records clarifying contract authorization, signature authority, site selection, relocation of the school-ground site, communications during apparent gaps in the timeline, and records concerning the Town’s later decision not to proceed. In light of the prior production, a conclusory statement that the Town does not maintain or possess responsive records is insufficient without identifying what offices, custodians, accounts, devices, and record systems were actually searched.

Accordingly, I request that the Town:

1. conduct or direct a renewed search of all offices and custodians reasonably likely to possess responsive records, including but not limited to the Supervisor’s Office, Town Clerk, Town Board, Town Attorney, Highway Department, Building Department, and any Town personnel or officials who communicated with Flock Safety or Pine Plains Police Department personnel regarding this matter;

2. search not only email accounts but also attachments, calendars, meeting records, texts, messages, handwritten notes, and records stored on personal devices or non-Town systems if used for Town business;

3. produce all responsive records located; or

4. if the Town continues to deny access, provide a more specific written explanation identifying:

   * the offices and custodians searched,
   * the record systems searched,
   * whether the Pine Plains Police Department was searched or is believed to possess responsive records,
   * whether any records were referred elsewhere internally rather than searched by the Town,
   * and the factual basis for claiming that the Town does not maintain or possess responsive records despite the earlier production.

Please also treat this appeal as a request that any appeal determination be transmitted to the Committee on Open Government as required by FOIL.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this appeal.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter
Independent Investigative Journalist

title: Austin Crime Free Housing Daily Notices

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Austin Crime Free Housing Daily Notices
communications.communication:
To Whom it may concern,
Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:
1. Any and all training materials used by the Austin, PD for their Crime Free Training Program (https://www.austintexas.gov/page/austin-crime-free-multi-housing-pilot-program) 
2. Total budget analysis for implementing and maintaining the Austin Crime Free Housing Pilot Program 
3. Copies of the daily notices sent to property managers about crime on their properties
4. Number of lease terminations / evictions since program enactment. 

Kindly,
Jenny Wadhwa

communications.subject: Public Information Act Request :: R183195-040626
communications.communication:
Dear Jenny Wadhwa: 
 
Thank you for your interest in the public records of the Austin Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 4/6/2026 and given the reference number R183195-040626 for tracking purposes. 
Please be advised that the Austin Police Department’s Central Records Division receives over 2500 requests for information a month. These requests are processed in the order received, and we strive to respond to each request promptly, as required by law. 
Please know that, as a result of the large volume of requests received, the anticipated timeframe for responding to your request may take 10 business days if it is simply an offense report you're requesting.  If you are requesting body cam, dash cam, or other related records your request could take up to 30 days. 
You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records requested. PLEASE NOTE: The Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Austin Public Records Center. 
Austin Police Department

communications.subject: [Austin Public Records Center] :: R183195-040626
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Re: Public Information Request of April 06, 2026, Reference # R183195-040626; 
Dear Jenny Wadhwa, 
The Austin Police Department received a Public Information request from you on April 06, 2026, to request copies of records pertaining to the following: “:To Whom it may concern, 
Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 
1. Any and all training materials used by the Austin, PD for their Crime Free Training Program (https://www.austintexas.gov/page/austin-crime-free-multi-housing-pilot-program) 
2. Total budget analysis for implementing and maintaining the Austin Crime Free Housing Pilot Program 
3. Copies of the daily notices sent to property managers about crime on their properties 
4. Number of lease terminations / evictions since program enactment. 

Kindly, 
Jenny Wadhwa 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2259227/” 
The City of Austin has responsive information for your request.  Please log in to the Open Records Center, at the following link to download the responsive records. Austin Police Department - R183195-040626 Please be advised the website only allows for two downloads of your files, once you have opened your files please save a copy for your records. Please note, no APD budget is tied to the Crime Free Multi-Housing Program specifically. The program itself is free, and any associated expenses are funded through various sources. Regarding the request for eviction data, the Austin Police Department plays no role in the eviction process, as evictions are civil matters. No eviction data is collected as part of the Crime Free Multi-Housing Program. Any eviction decisions are strictly between property ownership, management, and the tenant(s). 
Our portal works best on a computer using Google Chrome. Please ensure your device is allowing popups from our website. The downloads will open in a new tab or window that does not always pop in front of the window you are currently viewing. 
Thank you for contacting the City of Austin. 
Open Records Unit 
Austin Police Department

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: San Rafael Police Department Human Trafficking
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents produced between January 1st, 2023 until March 16th 2026 that include the term "human trafficking" "Eighteenth Street Gang" "Nortenos" "Mafia" "Nuestra Familia" "homeless" "unhoused" produced by the San Rafael police department. 

All police reports pertaining to human trafficking produced between January 1st, 2023 until March 16th 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: §19(b)(1) disclosures filed by Town Manager 2022-present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request:

1. All written disclosures filed by Town Manager Derek Brindisi under M.G.L. c. 268A §19(b)(1) from March 2022 to the present.
2. All written determinations issued by the Select Board under M.G.L. c. 268A §19(b)(1) regarding any disclosure filed by Mr. Brindisi during that period.
3. Specifically, any §19(b)(1) disclosure and determination concerning Mr. Brindisi's directorship of the Plymouth Regional Economic Development Foundation Inc and his participation as Town Manager in matters affecting that Foundation, including but not limited to the May 12, 2026 sale of the Joseph Simes House under Bid 22515.

If any portion is exempt, please identify the specific exemption claimed and produce the segregable portions. Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

communications.subject: Request# 2026-0919 : Request Submission Acceptance Confirmation
communications.communication:
Please DO NOT reply to this email !! Please use the email address of the sender at bottom of this email, in order to communicate with the request management team. 
Town of Plymouth, MA 
Public Record Request Number:2026-0919 
Requester: Edward Havey 
Request Date: Wednesday, May 27, 2026 7:30:00 AM 
Response Due Date: Wednesday, June 10, 2026 

Dear  Edward Havey : 

We have received your public Records request for Town of Plymouth. This record is being assigned to appropriate department. The department reviewer will review the request and create an effort estimate to create the response. Per state mandate, for Town of Plymouth, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will create a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will start working on your request. 

In case the estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that state mandate allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. We will do our due diligence towards this effort. However, the due date may be extended for any of the following reasons: 
1.   Any clarification that is required from you towards this request will stop the clock. The clock will be resumed once the proper clarification is received. 
2.   Payment of estimated effort – The time it takes to receive the due amount will be added to the original due date. 
3.    If in the opinion of the reviewer it is determined that original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort, the due date shall be extended based on reviewer’s judgment. You will be promptly notified in such case with revised due date. 
Thank you. 
Connor Anderson 
Records Access Officer 
Town of Plymouth 
26 Court St. 
Plymouth, MA 02360 
Tel: (508)-322-3388 x10213 
Email: canderson@plymouth-ma.gov

Under the Public Records Regulations, all appeals to the Supervisor must include a copy of the original request, any response by the RAO and a statement indicating the reason for the appeal. The requester must also provide a copy of the appeal petition to the RAO. By law, the Supervisor is required to respond within 10 business days of receipt of your appeal. In accordance with G.L. c. 66, § 10A(c), you have the right to seek judicial review of an unfavorable decision by commencing a civil action in the Superior Court.

title: Draft RCRA permit application for Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF)

communications.subject: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Draft RCRA permit application for Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

The draft Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) permit application submitted for the Savannah River Site Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF). The contractor, Savannah River Nuclear Solutions, told me the draft RCRA permit application was submitted in January.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes

title: Freedom of Information Act Request: DISB Consumer Complaint DC ID #2059580 (Shahidah Williams)

communications.subject: DC Freedom of Information Act Request: Freedom of Information Act Request: DISB Consumer Complaint DC ID #2059580 (Shahidah Williams)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

SUBJECT: Freedom of Information Act Request: DISB Consumer Complaint DCID #2059580 (Shahidah Williams)

To the FOIA Officer, Department of Insurance, Securities and Banking (DISB):

Pursuant to the District of Columbia Freedom of Information Act (D.C. Code § 2-531 et seq.), I am requesting expedited access to the following public records regarding a consumer complaint I filed against City Lending, Inc. in the fall of 2019. 

The complaint was assigned DCID #2059580 and was handled by Consumer Services Specialist Shahidah S. Williams, JD, within the Compliance Analysis Division. 

On November 6, 2019, Ms. Williams stated in writing that she was administratively closing my complaint and promised that an "official letter" would be generated. However, the core issues of predatory payoff escalations and the withholding of monthly statements were never resolved by your agency.

I demand the immediate production of the following precise records generated between September 1, 2019, and December 31, 2019:

1. The Official Closeout Letter: The formal administrative closeout letter referenced by Shahidah Williams on November 6, 2019, regarding the termination of complaint DCID #2059580.
2. Investigative Notes & Justification: All internal memoranda, case notes, or communications authored by Shahidah Williams justifying the closure of this complaint while severe discrepancies remained between the payoff provided by City Lending and the escalated payoff provided by Samuel I. White, P.C.
3. Supervisory Authorization: All records showing supervisory review and approval authorizing Shahidah Williams to administratively close DCID #2059580.
4. City Lending Communications: All emails, letters, and notes of telephone calls between Shahidah Williams and any representative of City Lending, Inc. (specifically Jorge Campodonico or Stephanie Simmons) regarding their failure to provide the consumer with monthly billing statements and their refusal to update the consumer's mailing address.
5. Escalation & Disciplinary Action (Predatory Payoff): All records, internal memoranda, and enforcement referrals generated by Shahidah Williams, her supervisors, or the Compliance Analysis Division detailing how the initial fabricated payoff amounts submitted by City Lending were escalated for regulatory review, and any subsequent disciplinary or enforcement actions taken against the lender.

I request these records be provided in their native, electronic format with all metadata intact. Because these records involve documented regulatory abandonment and subsequent financial harm to a District resident, I expect a comprehensive search that goes beyond a surface-level query.

Please provide a Vaughn Index for any documents or portions of documents that you claim are exempt from disclosure. 

Sincerely,

Fred Gamble Jr.
1490 Morris Rd SE
Washington, DC 20020

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

FRED GAMBLE JR

communications.communication:
Dear Fred Gamble: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of District of Columbia. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 5/6/2026 and given the reference number R008209-050626 for tracking purposes. 
In all future correspondence regarding this request, please reference request number R008209-050626. You can monitor the progress of your request in the " My Records Center". (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXcOhwwBPGHifqhO6OQOqgdwS-2FNpGxLbyeC-2FXmT4cIxy2XpmO_0kh4R9CdnlyZ11aPxhANbD5I-2FFIrYLukzpuygY3neOP0GqQntIG0OR6cMcoTco96Nfm3mYf98dy5SL7yrEKf2JV5FKB3cn8o4BYSwiRrEaoUByuyseOOEyfdSci-2BYbKKZ6LFGIje3bpkPA2UWXTRg4lymGhZUVLrGseUYUmyOk6t7yrlz6s2I4R5IqjsqcLczJ8pIweUx03lL14KdwGc5BZg5e-2B1zN-2BY5GoTW0Pf5yH2tYfYleBGnLHoGm8lGVu6AjEG3duhILrmM8oD-2Bx9H3dKcUH7-2FCNCc3ZN9Yw9PBHy0E-2BGZ5PvD8MKqgkj10HPLgvs2UZLqrcriFKd1FofKLfT5ueJB7KTtY1qORjec3LVVTT7Xg1cC0oZhwTpbOoSj) 
 
The District of Columbia is required to respond to your FOIA request within fifteen (15) business days of its receipt.  See D.C. Official Code § 2-532(c)(2011).  However, under certain circumstances, D.C. Official Code § 2-532(d)(2011) authorizes the District to extend, by ten (10) additional days, the deadline by which it must respond to a FOIA request.  One of the circumstances provided by that statute is a case in which the District needs additional time to search for, collect and examine voluminous amounts of records and/or consult with an additional D.C. agency. 
For any questions regarding your request, please contact the FOIA office directly. Find the list of agency Open Government and FOIA contact information by visiting FOIA Contact Information. For ease of reference, we ask that you have your FOIA Reference Number available when you contact the FOIA office. 
Regards, 
District of Columbia
